groups.label: expanding
groups.description: Paper-protocol expanding context, vehicle_then_aquarium direction, random ordering #5.

sentences.text: Infantry soldiers used the tank as a mobile shield while they crossed the open ground.

Infantry soldiers used the tank as a mobile shield while they crossed the open ground. The hydraulic turret drive enables the tank to slew its main gun three hundred sixty degrees in under nine seconds.

Infantry soldiers used the tank as a mobile shield while they crossed the open ground. The hydraulic turret drive enables the tank to slew its main gun three hundred sixty degrees in under nine seconds. Battlefield data links allow the tank to share targeting information in real time with other armored units.

Infantry soldiers used the tank as a mobile shield while they crossed the open ground. The hydraulic turret drive enables the tank to slew its main gun three hundred sixty degrees in under nine seconds. Battlefield data links allow the tank to share targeting information in real time with other armored units. Satellite imagery revealed a freshly dug revetment designed to conceal one tank from aerial observation.

Infantry soldiers used the tank as a mobile shield while they crossed the open ground. The hydraulic turret drive enables the tank to slew its main gun three hundred sixty degrees in under nine seconds. Battlefield data links allow the tank to share targeting information in real time with other armored units. Satellite imagery revealed a freshly dug revetment designed to conceal one tank from aerial observation. Reactive armor tiles mounted on the tank hull detonate outward to neutralize incoming anti-armor projectiles.

Infantry soldiers used the tank as a mobile shield while they crossed the open ground. The hydraulic turret drive enables the tank to slew its main gun three hundred sixty degrees in under nine seconds. Battlefield data links allow the tank to share targeting information in real time with other armored units. Satellite imagery revealed a freshly dug revetment designed to conceal one tank from aerial observation. Reactive armor tiles mounted on the tank hull detonate outward to neutralize incoming anti-armor projectiles. The fire control system allows the tank to engage targets accurately while moving at full speed.

The fire control system allows the tank to engage targets accurately while moving at full speed. The lieutenant climbed out of the tank and surveyed the landscape through a pair of field glasses.

Urban warfare doctrine recommends that infantry always accompany the tank to guard against close-range threats. An improvised explosive device detonated beneath the lead tank but the reinforced hull absorbed most of the blast. An enemy rocket struck the side armor of the tank but failed to penetrate the hull.
